text:
The vain mind, trapped within its own self-praise,
Ignores the wind that gives the grass its grace.

From festive cups, seek no delight to chase;
The prize has fled the trap of time and place.

His mercy may accept—what is more true?
The best appeal against your past disgrace
Is shame that leaves no argument in place.

The heart itself, for those who live in pain,
Is but a blister underfoot; what trace
Of fear can hardship find upon their face?
